2603.24535.title: Representation Learning to Study Temporal Dynamics in Tutorial Scaffolding
2603.24535.paper_summary: Adaptive scaffolding enhances learning, yet the field lacks robust methods for measuring it within authentic tutoring dialogue. This gap has become more pressing with the rise of remote human tutoring and large language model-based systems. We introduce an embedding-based approach that analyzes scaffolding dynamics by aligning the semantics of dialogue turns, problem statements, and correct solutions. Specifically, we operationalize alignment by computing cosine similarity between tutor and student contributions and task-relevant content. We apply this framework to 1,576 real-world mathematics tutoring dialogues from the Eedi Question Anchored Tutoring Dialogues dataset. The analysis reveals systematic differences in task alignment and distinct temporal patterns in how participants ground their contributions in problem and solution content. Further, mixed-effects models show that role-specific semantic alignment predicts tutorial progression beyond baseline features such as message order and length. Tutor contributions exhibited stronger grounding in problem content early in interactions. In contrast, student solution alignment was modestly positively associated with progression. These findings support scaffolding as a continuous, role-sensitive process grounded in task semantics. By capturing role-specific alignment over time, this approach provides a principled method for analyzing instructional dialogue and evaluating conversational tutoring systems.
2603.24535.paper_summary_zh: 適應性支架增強了學習，但該領域缺乏在真實輔導對話中測量它的穩健方法。隨著遠程人類輔導和基於大型語言模型的系統的興起，這一空白變得更加迫切。我們介紹了一種基於嵌入的方法，通過對齊對話回合、問題陳述和正確解決方案的語義來分析支架動態。具體而言，我們通過計算導師和學生貢獻及任務相關內容之間的餘弦相似度來實現對齊。我們將這一框架應用於來自Eedi問題錨定輔導對話數據集的1,576個現實世界數學輔導對話。分析顯示任務對齊的系統性差異以及參與者在如何將其貢獻與問題和解決方案內容相結合的獨特時間模式。此外，混合效應模型顯示，角色特定的語義對齊預測了教學進展，超出了消息順序和長度等基線特徵。導師的貢獻在互動早期表現出更強的問題內容基礎。相反，學生的解決方案對齊與進展之間的關聯適度正向。這些發現支持支架作為一個持續的、角色敏感的過程，基於任務語義。通過捕捉隨時間變化的角色特定對齊，這一方法提供了一種原則性的方法來分析教學對話並評估對話輔導系統。
2603.24535.author: Conrad Borchers et.al.
2603.24535.authors: Conrad Borchers, Jiayi Zhang, Ashish Gurung
